notes: Spec-lock: three new modules (alerts.py bus + alerts_sse.py transport + alerts_telegram.py bridge) + one new page (ALERTS_PAGE) + [alerts.telegram] config block + 4 new endpoints. Reuses existing [telegram] bot_token / chat_id from platform.toml -- F014 does NOT add a new secret. Zero imports from agent/live/*, agent/risk/*, agent/squad/*. Sprint 2 does NOT publish events from any live pathway (D065).
invariants_pinned: Ring buffer bounded (100 events; oldest fall off silently)
Subscriber exceptions never break other subscribers (test-pinned)
Six-event-type whitelist; adding a new type requires Legal re-review
SSE frame shape matches WHATWG spec (id/event/data + blank line)
Telegram bridge fail-closed: enabled AND bot_token AND chat_id all populated
load_config() NEVER echoes raw bot_token or chat_id -- only boolean flags
SSE stream exempt from rate limiter (long-lived connection would trickle-drain the bucket)
No real Telegram call in tests (httpx mocked via _FakeClient)
acceptance: 35 tests: 10 bus module + 5 SSE module + 8 Telegram module + 7 page + 5 API
SSE live-delivery integration test on tape
Telegram httpx.post mocked; per_event filter respected; disabled config -> no post
